sanskrit:
समः शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयोः।
शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु समः सङ्गविवर्जितः॥
तुल्यनिन्दास्तुतिर्मौनी सन्तुष्टो येन केनचित्।
अनिकेतः स्थिरमतिर्भक्तिमान्मे प्रियो नरः॥
transliteration:
samaḥ śatrau ca mitre ca tathā mānāpamānayoḥ |
śītoṣṇa-sukha-duḥkheṣu samaḥ saṅga-vivarjitaḥ ||
tulya-nindā-stutir maunī santuṣṭo yena kenacit |
aniketaḥ sthira-matir bhaktimān me priyo naraḥ ||
literal_meaning: Equal toward foe and friend, likewise to honor and dishonor; same in cold-heat, pleasure-pain, fame-infamy; free from clinging; content with whatever comes; homeless, steady-minded, devoted—such a person is dear to Me.
contextual_meaning: Krishna sketches the psychological profile of the securely-attached devotee whose self-worth is no longer outsourced to people, possessions, or reputations; affect-regulation is internal, so social feedback loops lose their sting.
psychological_state: Neuroceptive safety: nervous system defaults to ventral-vagal tone; triggers are noticed, labeled, and released without defensive story-making.
trigger_scenario: Your post is ignored on social media while a rival’s goes viral; you notice the sting, breathe, and return to writing without spiraling into self-doubt or retaliation.
anchor_text: Let praise and blame, gain and loss, feel the same to your steady heart.
